audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's demand to argue his case before God, rebuke of friends as whitewashers/false healers, legal-risk language, and plea for God to remove terror and disclose charges.

verses.restored: Look, my eye has seen all this; my ear has heard and understood it.
What you know, I also know; I do not fall below you.
But I would speak to Shaddai, and I desire to argue with the Divine.
But you are smearers of falsehood; all of you are worthless healers.
If only you would be completely silent; that would become your wisdom.
Please hear my argument, and listen to the pleadings of my lips.
Will you speak injustice for the Divine, and speak deceit for him?
Will you lift his face? Will you contend for the Divine?
Will it be good when he searches you out? Or can you deceive him as one deceives a mortal?
He will surely rebuke you if in secret you lift faces.
Will not his majesty terrify you, and his dread fall upon you?
Your memorable sayings are proverbs of ash; your defenses are defenses of clay.
Be silent from me, and I myself will speak; let whatever may pass over me pass.
Why should I take my flesh in my teeth and put my life in my palm?
Though he slay me, I will wait/hope for him; yet I will argue my ways to his face.
This also will be my salvation, for a godless person cannot come before him.
Hear carefully my word, and let my declaration be in your ears.
Look now, I have arranged my case; I know that I myself am right.
Who is he who will contend with me? For then I would be silent and expire.
Only two things do not do to me; then I will not hide myself from your face:
Remove your hand far from me, and let your terror not frighten me.
Then call, and I will answer; or let me speak, and you reply to me.
How many are my iniquities and sins? Make me know my transgression and my sin.
Why do you hide your face and count me as your enemy?
Will you terrify a driven leaf and pursue dry stubble?
For you write bitter things against me and make me inherit the iniquities of my youth.
You put my feet in the stocks, watch all my paths, and trace the soles of my feet.
And he wears away like rot, like a garment eaten by moths.
